type: CLAIM

Proposing I own this file and implement both features. Please send a SPEC for fallback_searchpath (exact signature, behavior in get_source/list_templates, edge cases) and I'll implement it alongside path_transform.

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py

type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
spec: Add to FileSystemLoader ONLY (leave other loader classes to your feature):
1) __init__ signature: add param 'fallback_searchpath: t.Optional[t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]]] = None' after the existing 'searchpath' param (before encoding/followlinks, or as a keyword-only trailing param - either is fine as long as searchpath stays positional-first for backward compat). Process it exactly like searchpath is processed: if not isinstance(fallback_searchpath, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(fallback_searchpath, str): wrap in a list. Then self.fallback_searchpath = [os.fspath(p) for p in fallback_searchpath] if fallback_searchpath else []. If None, self.fallback_searchpath = [].
2) get_source(): current code loops 'for searchpath in self.searchpath: ...' and returns on first match, else raises TemplateNotFound. Change to loop over itertools.chain(self.searchpath, self.fallback_searchpath) (or simply concatenate self.searchpath + self.fallback_searchpath) in that order, using the identical existing lookup/open_if_exists/uptodate logic unchanged for every path in the combined list. First match wins (primary paths listed first so they win ties). Still raise TemplateNotFound(template) if no match anywhere.
3) list_templates(): currently loops 'for searchpath in self.searchpath: os.walk(...)' building a 'found' set. Change to iterate over self.searchpath + self.fallback_searchpath (primary first) with the same os.walk/dedup logic - the existing 'if template not in found' check already dedups so a template found in a primary path is what's kept if it appears again in a fallback path (since it's already in the set, it won't be re-added, and the primary one already determined the file that get_source would return). Return sorted(found) as before, unchanged.
4) Docstring: please add ':param fallback_searchpath:' entry describing it as directories searched only if the template isn't found in searchpath, and mention priority (primary always wins). No other behavior changes needed. This is fully backward compatible when fallback_searchpath is None/omitted.

type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
spec: Add optional path_transform param, defaulting to None, to loaders that resolve templates. Signature: path_transform: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str], str]] = None.

1) BaseLoader: no change needed to BaseLoader itself (it has no __init__ currently) EXCEPT its subclasses below each need the param. Do not add transform logic to BaseLoader.load() since that operates on 'name' already passed by caller/environment -- instead apply transform inside each loader's get_source (and load, for PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader which override load).

2) FileSystemLoader.__init__: add  as last kwarg (after followlinks, before/after your fallback_searchpath param -- order between our two new params doesn't matter, just keep both). Store as . In get_source(self, environment, template): at the very top, if self.path_transform is not None, compute  else . Use  for split_template_path(...) and all file resolution. If TemplateNotFound is raised (not found), raise it with the ORIGINAL  (not ) as the argument -- i.e.  at the end, not the transformed name. This applies whether or not fallback_searchpath kicks in.

3) DictLoader.__init__: add same  param, store . In get_source: compute ; look up  in self.mapping; on success return using  where a name is needed (mapping.get(lookup) in the uptodate closure); on failure raise TemplateNotFound(template) (original name).

4) FunctionLoader.__init__: add  param, store it. In get_source: compute  same way, call  instead of . On rv is None, raise TemplateNotFound(template) (original).

5) PackageLoader.__init__: add  param, store it. In get_source: compute  the same way and use it wherever  is currently used for path construction (split_template_path(lookup)). On not-found raise TemplateNotFound(template) (original).

6) PrefixLoader.__init__: add  param (applies to the FULL template string including prefix, transformed BEFORE splitting on the delimiter), store it. Add a small helper or inline: in get_loader(self, template): first compute , then do the prefix/delimiter split on  instead of , but keep raising  (original) on failure. Both get_source and load call get_loader so this covers both -- no other changes needed in get_source/load bodies since they already delegate via get_loader and re-raise using the original / on TemplateNotFound.

7) ChoiceLoader.__init__: add  param, store it. In get_source and load: compute  (in load, transform  into  similarly) ONCE at the top, then call  /  for each child loader in the loop, but on final failure raise TemplateNotFound(template) / TemplateNotFound(name) using the ORIGINAL untransformed value.

General rules: transform is applied exactly once at the top level before delegating to any child/physical lookup; composite loaders (PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader) apply their OWN transform before delegating to child loaders and do NOT re-apply the child loader's transform on top (children still apply their own transform if they have one, since they're independent loader instances -- this is fine, no special-casing needed). Any exception raised by path_transform itself should propagate uncaught. Empty string input to path_transform is passed through normally (loader does not special-case it). If path_transform is None, behavior is 100% identical to today (no wrapping, no extra function call).

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Our DEFERs crossed in flight - resolving now: I will own src/jinja2/loaders.py and implement both features. Your SPEC came through with inline-code spans stripped (e.g. 'add  as last kwarg', 'Store as .' are missing the actual variable/attr names). Please resend the SPEC using plain text without backticks/code-formatting so exact names like param name, attribute name, and local variable name for the transformed lookup string survive. Meanwhile I'll proceed with obvious inference (param name 'path_transform', attribute 'self.path_transform', local var e.g. 'lookup' or 'name') and adjust once you confirm.

type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
spec: Add optional path_transform param, defaulting to None, to loaders that resolve templates. Signature: path_transform: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str], str]] = None.

1) BaseLoader: no change needed to BaseLoader itself (it has no __init__ currently) EXCEPT its subclasses below each need the param. Do not add transform logic to BaseLoader.load() since that operates on 'name' already passed by caller/environment -- instead apply transform inside each loader's get_source (and load, for PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader which override load).

2) FileSystemLoader.__init__: add path_transform kwarg (Optional[Callable[[str], str]] = None) as last kwarg (after followlinks, order relative to your fallback_searchpath param doesn't matter, just keep both). Store as self.path_transform = path_transform. In get_source(self, environment, template): at the very top, if self.path_transform is not None, compute lookup = self.path_transform(template) else lookup = template. Use lookup for split_template_path(...) and all file resolution. If not found, raise TemplateNotFound with the ORIGINAL template (not lookup) as the argument -- i.e. raise TemplateNotFound(template) at the end, not the transformed name. This applies whether or not fallback_searchpath kicks in.

3) DictLoader.__init__: add same path_transform param, store self.path_transform. In get_source: compute lookup = self.path_transform(template) if self.path_transform else template; look up lookup in self.mapping; on success return using lookup where a name is needed (mapping.get(lookup) in the uptodate closure); on failure raise TemplateNotFound(template) (original name).

4) FunctionLoader.__init__: add path_transform param, store it. In get_source: compute lookup same way, call self.load_func(lookup) instead of self.load_func(template). On rv is None, raise TemplateNotFound(template) (original).

5) PackageLoader.__init__: add path_transform param, store it. In get_source: compute lookup the same way and use it wherever template is currently used for path construction (split_template_path(lookup)). On not-found raise TemplateNotFound(template) (original).

6) PrefixLoader.__init__: add path_transform param (applies to the FULL template string including prefix, transformed BEFORE splitting on the delimiter), store it. In get_loader(self, template): first compute lookup = self.path_transform(template) if self.path_transform else template, then do the prefix/delimiter split on lookup instead of template, but keep raising TemplateNotFound(template) (original) on failure. Both get_source and load call get_loader so this covers both -- no other changes needed in get_source/load bodies since they already delegate via get_loader and re-raise using the original template/name on TemplateNotFound.

7) ChoiceLoader.__init__: add path_transform param, store it. In get_source and load: compute lookup = self.path_transform(template) if self.path_transform else template (in load, transform name into local_name similarly) ONCE at the top, then call loader.get_source(environment, lookup) / loader.load(environment, local_name, globals) for each child loader in the loop, but on final failure raise TemplateNotFound(template) / TemplateNotFound(name) using the ORIGINAL untransformed value.

General rules: transform is applied exactly once at the top level before delegating to any child/physical lookup; composite loaders (PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader) apply their OWN transform before delegating to child loaders and do NOT re-apply the child loader's transform on top (children still apply their own transform if they have one, since they're independent loader instances -- this is fine, no special-casing needed). Any exception raised by path_transform itself should propagate uncaught. Empty string input to path_transform is passed through normally (loader does not special-case it). If path_transform is None, behavior is 100% identical to today (no wrapping, no extra function call).
